title: Axis Ticket Field
prompt:
Create a production-ready Axis Ticket Field using TypeScript React, Tailwind CSS, and Framer Motion. This original interactive background is a quiet ticket-like coordinate field with three named anchors: Intake, Review, and Release. A person selects an anchor and a small cyan ticket marker moves between sparse horizontal and vertical axis lines; a readable sentence names the current location. The background must work inside a compact card and a larger detail preview without reading as decoration only. Use a light Bento system: #F4F5F6 stage, white panel, #ECECEE border and line work, 22px corners, soft neutral shadow, #111111 type, and #4A4A4F secondary copy. Cyan is the only bright accent, and card surfaces must be solid rather than gradient-filled.

Use a labelled group of native 44px buttons with aria-pressed and an aria-live polite location message. Click, Enter, and Space select an anchor. Move the ticket marker with a spring using stiffness 280 and damping 25; use a 150ms ease-out opacity shift for the location copy. Keep interaction deterministic and local: no timers, random values, storage, remote assets, network requests, scroll listeners, browser globals, or layout reads. Use transform and opacity for the marker.

At 320px, keep the axis field and buttons readable without cropping; at detail size, allow more empty space around the calm grid. Provide visible 3px focus rings, logical order, AA contrast, and labels that do not depend on cyan. Respect prefers-reduced-motion by placing the marker immediately with only a short opacity change. Return complete client code, a pure CSS approximation, and suggested HTML.
demoCode:
(() => {function Demo(){const [a,setA]=React.useState(0);const x=[['Intake','12%','24%'],['Review','47%','52%'],['Release','76%','72%']];return

Ticket located at {x[a][0]}.

}return ;})()
